architecture.summary: VLM-Safe-RL integrates a frozen vision-language model (CLIP) into constrained Markov decision process (CMDP) optimization via PPO-Lagrangian. A frozen VLM encodes visual observations and outputs per-step anticipatory cost scores (via margin-based logistic confidence gating), which are injected into the Lagrange multiplier update alongside traditional post-hoc cost signals. Two independent pathways (Decoupled Dual-Path CLIP) separate reward and cost objectives; Confidence Gating applies Bayes-optimal weighting to the VLM cost term. The composed algorithm (VLMPPOLag) runs on top of a frozen vision-language backbone without retraining it.

biblio.title: Seeing Before Colliding: Anticipatory Safe RL with Frozen Vision-Language Models

baseline.name: PPOLag, CPO, CPPOPID, CPO-CLG, PPOLag-RND (five constraint-aware baselines)

claims.statement: VLMPPOLag+Conf is the only configuration in the default budget comparison that simultaneously retains substantive return (J_r ≈ 40) and holds cost within budget on a majority of seeds
catastrophe rate reduced from 41% to 26% on held-out MetaDrive Medium (95% bootstrap CI [-26, -5] pp)
The mechanism generalizes to held-out MetaDrive Medium and shows directionally consistent transfer to Bullet Safety-Gym
The approach does not transfer to MetaDrive Easy/Hard and fails with Qwen2-VL backbone
The MetaDrive Hard failure is traced to a Lagrangian-regulation pathology rather than the VLM signal itself
Decoupled Dual-Path CLIP maintains independent reward and cost paths that respect the CMDP's factorization
Confidence Gating derives a Bayes-optimal weight from a logistic noise model on the CLIP margin
This is the first work to use frozen VLM signals as an anticipatory cost term inside the CMDP Lagrangian update
